creator: wietse@porcupine.org
text:
With virtualbox-ose-3.1.2, Solaris10 does not properly come up from
the install CDROM, which is needed for installing the OS, and for
making emergency repairs to an installed OS.

With virtualbox-3.0.51.r22902_1, I could boot from CDROM and
reconfigure a Solaris10 guest machine that was originally created
under VMware, and run it on VirtualBox.

Losing the ability to properly run Solaris is a surprising regression,
for software whose development was sponsored by SUN.

How-To-Repeat: Boot from Solaris10 CDROM. Let it autodetect the hardware without
user input. In response to "Select type of installation" enter "b
-s". This is the emergency repair mode to reconfigure a system after
its (real or virtual) hardware has changed.

The system comes up in single user mode:

    Requesting System Maintenance Mode
    SINGLE USER MODE
    #

It then ignores all keyboard input. After several minutes, the
"interactive installation" procedure kicks in and it also ignores
all keyboard input. See logfile below for the VirtualBox view of
events.

(Of course one can also select "interactive installation" mode
directly. After a series of error messages, this also ignores all
keyboard input).
